claim: Approximately 22,500 people are infected with human T-cell lymphotropic virus type 1 in the United Kingdom.

result.claim: Approximately 22,500 people are infected with human T-cell lymphotropic virus type 1 in the United Kingdom.
result.reasoning: My searches for the number of people infected with human T-cell lymphotropic virus type 1 (HTLV-1) in the United Kingdom, as well as the prevalence of HTLV-1 in the UK, did not yield any relevant information. The search results provided data on other viruses, diseases, and genetic mutations, but no specific figures or studies pertaining to HTLV-1 in the United Kingdom were found in the knowledge base. Therefore, there is insufficient evidence to support or refute the claim.
